text: Wild greens sautéed and simmered in a tangy tomatillo sauce, often served with beans and handmade tortillas. A simple, nutritious dish with deep regional roots.
Tender beef or pork cooked with purslane and seasoned with local herbs like chivatito. It’s a bold, aromatic stew that showcases the flavors of the highlands.
A soft, slightly sweet bread made with pulque, the traditional fermented agave drink. Its unique flavor and fluffy texture make it a local favorite during fairs and family gatherings.
